an6.63:1.1: "I will teach you, *bhikkhus*, a penetrating exposition of the *Dhamma*.
an6.63:1.2: Listen to it, pay careful attention, and I will speak."
an6.63:10.2: Contact, *bhikkhus*, is the origin of sensual pleasures.
an6.63:11.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the diversity of sensual pleasures?
an6.63:11.2: Different, *bhikkhus*, is the sensual pleasure with regard to forms; different the sensual pleasure with regard to sounds; different the sensual pleasure with regard to scents; different the sensual pleasure with regard to tastes; different the sensual pleasure with regard to tangibles.
an6.63:11.3: This, *bhikkhus*, is called the diversity of sensual pleasures.
an6.63:12.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the result of sensual pleasures?
an6.63:12.2: Whatever mode of existence one who desires generates again and again — whether of a meritorious or demeritorious kind — this, *bhikkhus*, is called the result of sensual pleasures.
an6.63:13.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the cessation of sensual pleasures?
an6.63:13.2: The cessation of contact, *bhikkhus*, is the cessation of sensual pleasures.
an6.63:13.3: This very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of sensual pleasures, namely —
an6.63:13.4: right view, right intention, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right *sati*, right *samādhi*.
an6.63:14.1: When, *bhikkhus*, the noble disciple understands sensual pleasures thus, understands the origin of sensual pleasures thus, understands the diversity of sensual pleasures thus, understands the result of sensual pleasures thus, understands the cessation of sensual pleasures thus, understands the path leading to the cessation of sensual pleasures thus — he understands this penetrating holy life as the cessation of sensual pleasures.
an6.63:14.2: 'Sensual pleasures, *bhikkhus*, are to be understood … the path leading to the cessation of sensual pleasures is to be understood' —
an6.63:14.3: that is what was said, and this was said with reference to that.
an6.63:15.1: '*Vedanā*, *bhikkhus*, is to be understood … the path leading to the cessation of *vedanā* is to be understood' —
an6.63:15.2: so it was said. With reference to what was this said?
an6.63:16.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the origin of *vedanā*?
an6.63:17.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the diversity of *vedanā*?
an6.63:17.2: There is, *bhikkhus*, worldly pleasant *vedanā*, there is unworldly pleasant *vedanā*; there is worldly painful *vedanā*, there is unworldly painful *vedanā*; there is worldly neither-painful-nor-pleasant *vedanā*, there is unworldly neither-painful-nor-pleasant *vedanā*.
an6.63:17.3: This, *bhikkhus*, is called the diversity of *vedanā*.
an6.63:18.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the result of *vedanā*?
an6.63:18.2: Whatever mode of existence one who feels generates again and again — whether of a meritorious or demeritorious kind —
an6.63:18.3: this, *bhikkhus*, is called the result of *vedanā*.
an6.63:19.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the cessation of *vedanā*?
an6.63:19.2: The cessation of contact, *bhikkhus*, is the cessation of *vedanā*.
an6.63:19.3: This very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of *vedanā*, namely —
an6.63:2.1: "And what, *bhikkhus*, is this penetrating exposition of the *Dhamma*?
an6.63:2.2: Sensual pleasures, *bhikkhus*, are to be understood; the origin of sensual pleasures is to be understood; the diversity of sensual pleasures is to be understood; the result of sensual pleasures is to be understood; the cessation of sensual pleasures is to be understood; the path leading to the cessation of sensual pleasures is to be understood.
an6.63:20.1: When, *bhikkhus*, the noble disciple understands *vedanā* thus, understands the origin of *vedanā* thus, understands the diversity of *vedanā* thus, understands the result of *vedanā* thus, understands the cessation of *vedanā* thus, understands the path leading to the cessation of *vedanā* thus — he understands this penetrating holy life as the cessation of *vedanā*.
an6.63:20.2: '*Vedanā*, *bhikkhus*, is to be understood … the path leading to the cessation of *vedanā* is to be understood' —
an6.63:20.3: that is what was said, and this was said with reference to that.
an6.63:21.1: '*Saññā*, *bhikkhus*, is to be understood … the path leading to the cessation of *saññā* is to be understood' —
an6.63:21.2: so it was said. With reference to what was this said?
an6.63:21.4: *saññā* of forms, *saññā* of sounds, *saññā* of scents, *saññā* of tastes, *saññā* of tangibles, *saññā* of *dhammas*.
an6.63:22.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the origin of *saññā*?
an6.63:23.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the diversity of *saññā*?
an6.63:23.2: Different, *bhikkhus*, is the *saññā* with regard to forms; different the *saññā* with regard to sounds; different the *saññā* with regard to scents; different the *saññā* with regard to tastes; different the *saññā* with regard to tangibles; different the *saññā* with regard to *dhammas*.
an6.63:23.3: This, *bhikkhus*, is called the diversity of *saññā*.
an6.63:24.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the result of *saññā*?
an6.63:24.2: I say, *bhikkhus*, that *saññā* has verbal expression as its result.
an6.63:24.3: However one perceives something, one speaks of it accordingly, saying: 'I perceived it thus.'
an6.63:24.4: This, *bhikkhus*, is called the result of *saññā*.
an6.63:25.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the cessation of *saññā*?
an6.63:25.2: The cessation of contact, *bhikkhus*, is the cessation of *saññā*.
an6.63:25.3: This very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of *saññā*, namely —
an6.63:26.1: When, *bhikkhus*, the noble disciple understands *saññā* thus, understands the origin of *saññā* thus, understands the diversity of *saññā* thus, understands the result of *saññā* thus, understands the cessation of *saññā* thus, understands the path leading to the cessation of *saññā* thus — he understands this penetrating holy life as the cessation of *saññā*.
an6.63:26.2: '*Saññā*, *bhikkhus*, is to be understood … the path leading to the cessation of *saññā* is to be understood'.
an6.63:26.3: That is what was said, and this was said with reference to that.
an6.63:27.1: '*Āsavas*, *bhikkhus*, are to be understood … the path leading to the cessation of the *āsavas* is to be understood' —
an6.63:27.2: so it was said. With reference to what was this said?
an6.63:27.4: the *āsava* of sensuality, the *āsava* of *bhava*, the *āsava* of blindness [*avijjā*].
an6.63:28.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the origin of the *āsavas*?
an6.63:28.2: Blindness [*avijjā*], *bhikkhus*, is the origin of the *āsavas*.
an6.63:29.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the diversity of the *āsavas*?
an6.63:29.2: There are, *bhikkhus*, *āsavas* leading to hell; *āsavas* leading to the animal realm; *āsavas* leading to the ghost realm; *āsavas* leading to the human world; *āsavas* leading to the divine world.
an6.63:29.3: This, *bhikkhus*, is called the diversity of the *āsavas*.
an6.63:3.1: *Vedanā*, *bhikkhus*, is to be understood; the origin of *vedanā* is to be understood; the diversity of *vedanā* is to be understood; the result of *vedanā* is to be understood; the cessation of *vedanā* is to be understood; the path leading to the cessation of *vedanā* is to be understood.
an6.63:30.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the result of the *āsavas*?
an6.63:30.2: Whatever mode of existence one who has gone into blindness [*avijjā*] generates again and again — whether of a meritorious or demeritorious kind — this, *bhikkhus*, is called the result of the *āsavas*.
an6.63:31.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the cessation of the *āsavas*?
an6.63:31.2: The cessation of blindness [*avijjā*], *bhikkhus*, is the cessation of the *āsavas*.
an6.63:31.3: This very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of the *āsavas*, namely —
an6.63:32.1: When, *bhikkhus*, the noble disciple understands the *āsavas* thus, understands the origin of the *āsavas* thus, understands the diversity of the *āsavas* thus, understands the result of the *āsavas* thus, understands the cessation of the *āsavas* thus, understands the path leading to the cessation of the *āsavas* thus — he understands this penetrating holy life as the cessation of the *āsavas*.
an6.63:32.2: '*Āsavas*, *bhikkhus*, are to be understood … the path leading to the cessation of the *āsavas* is to be understood'.
an6.63:32.3: That is what was said, and this was said with reference to that.
an6.63:33.1: '*Kamma*, *bhikkhus*, is to be understood … the path leading to the cessation of *kamma* is to be understood' — so it was said.
an6.63:33.2: With reference to what was this said?
an6.63:34.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the origin of *kamma*?
an6.63:35.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the diversity of *kamma*?
an6.63:35.2: There is, *bhikkhus*, *kamma* to be experienced in hell; *kamma* to be experienced in the animal realm; *kamma* to be experienced in the ghost realm; *kamma* to be experienced in the human world; *kamma* to be experienced in the divine world.
an6.63:35.3: This, *bhikkhus*, is called the diversity of *kamma*.
an6.63:36.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the result of *kamma*?
an6.63:36.2: I say, *bhikkhus*, that the result of *kamma* is threefold —
an6.63:36.3: in this very life, upon rebirth, or at some later time.
an6.63:36.4: This, *bhikkhus*, is called the result of *kamma*.
an6.63:37.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the cessation of *kamma*?
an6.63:37.2: The cessation of contact, *bhikkhus*, is the cessation of *kamma*.
an6.63:37.3: This very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of *kamma*, namely —
an6.63:38.1: When, *bhikkhus*, the noble disciple understands *kamma* thus, understands the origin of *kamma* thus, understands the diversity of *kamma* thus, understands the result of *kamma* thus, understands the cessation of *kamma* thus, understands the path leading to the cessation of *kamma* thus — he understands this penetrating holy life as the cessation of *kamma*.
an6.63:38.2: '*Kamma*, *bhikkhus*, is to be understood … the path leading to the cessation of *kamma* is to be understood' —
an6.63:38.3: that is what was said, and this was said with reference to that.
an6.63:39.1: *Dukkha*, *bhikkhus*, is to be understood; the origin of *dukkha* is to be understood; the diversity of *dukkha* is to be understood; the result of *dukkha* is to be understood; the cessation of *dukkha* is to be understood; the path leading to the cessation of *dukkha* is to be understood.
an6.63:39.2: So it was said. With reference to what was this said?
an6.63:39.3: Birth is *dukkha*, aging is *dukkha*, illness is *dukkha*, death is *dukkha*, sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief, and despair are *dukkha*, not getting what one wishes is *dukkha*; in brief, the five clinging [*upādāna*] aggregates are *dukkha*.
an6.63:4.1: *Saññā*, *bhikkhus*, is to be understood; the origin of *saññā* is to be understood; the diversity of *saññā* is to be understood; the result of *saññā* is to be understood; the cessation of *saññā* is to be understood; the path leading to the cessation of *saññā* is to be understood.
an6.63:40.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the origin of *dukkha*?
an6.63:41.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the diversity of *dukkha*?
an6.63:41.2: There is, *bhikkhus*, intense *dukkha*, there is mild *dukkha*; there are those who are slow to become dispassionate, there are those who are quick to become dispassionate.
an6.63:41.3: This, *bhikkhus*, is called the diversity of *dukkha*.
an6.63:42.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the result of *dukkha*?
an6.63:42.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, someone overwhelmed by *dukkha*, their mind consumed, grieves, is distressed, laments, beats their breast and weeps, falls into confusion; or else, overwhelmed by *dukkha*, their mind consumed, they set out on an external search:
an6.63:42.3: 'Who knows a charm or a verse for the cessation of this *dukkha*?'
an6.63:42.4: I say, *bhikkhus*, that *dukkha* results in confusion or in searching.
an6.63:42.5: This, *bhikkhus*, is called the result of *dukkha*.
an6.63:43.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the cessation of *dukkha*?
an6.63:43.2: The cessation of *taṇhā*, *bhikkhus*, is the cessation of *dukkha*.
an6.63:43.3: This very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of *dukkha*, namely —
an6.63:44.1: When, *bhikkhus*, the noble disciple understands *dukkha* thus, understands the origin of *dukkha* thus, understands the diversity of *dukkha* thus, understands the result of *dukkha* thus, understands the cessation of *dukkha* thus, understands the path leading to the cessation of *dukkha* thus — he understands this penetrating holy life as the cessation of *dukkha*.
an6.63:44.2: *Dukkha*, *bhikkhus*, is to be understood; the origin of *dukkha* is to be understood; the diversity of *dukkha* is to be understood; the result of *dukkha* is to be understood; the cessation of *dukkha* is to be understood; the path leading to the cessation of *dukkha* is to be understood.
an6.63:44.3: That is what was said, and this was said with reference to that.
an6.63:45.1: This, *bhikkhus*, is the penetrating exposition of the *Dhamma*."
an6.63:5.1: *Āsavas*, *bhikkhus*, are to be understood; the origin of the *āsavas* is to be understood; the diversity of the *āsavas* is to be understood; the result of the *āsavas* is to be understood; the cessation of the *āsavas* is to be understood; the path leading to the cessation of the *āsavas* is to be understood.
an6.63:6.1: *Kamma*, *bhikkhus*, is to be understood; the origin of *kamma* is to be understood; the diversity of *kamma* is to be understood; the result of *kamma* is to be understood; the cessation of *kamma* is to be understood; the path leading to the cessation of *kamma* is to be understood.
an6.63:7.1: *Dukkha*, *bhikkhus*, is to be understood; the origin of *dukkha* is to be understood; the diversity of *dukkha* is to be understood; the result of *dukkha* is to be understood; the cessation of *dukkha* is to be understood; the path leading to the cessation of *dukkha* is to be understood.
an6.63:8.1: 'Sensual pleasures, *bhikkhus*, are to be understood; the origin of sensual pleasures is to be understood; the diversity of sensual pleasures is to be understood; the result of sensual pleasures is to be understood; the cessation of sensual pleasures is to be understood; the path leading to the cessation of sensual pleasures is to be understood' — so it was said.
an6.63:8.10: A person's sensual pleasure is the passion of intention,
an6.63:8.11: Not these beautiful things of the world;
an6.63:8.12: A person's sensual pleasure is the passion of intention,
an6.63:8.13: The beautiful things remain just as they are in the world;
an6.63:8.14: But here the wise discipline away desire.
an6.63:8.2: With reference to what was this said?
an6.63:8.4: forms cognizable by the eye that are wished for, desired, agreeable, pleasant in nature, connected with sensuality, enticing;
an6.63:8.8: tangibles cognizable by the body that are wished for, desired, agreeable, pleasant in nature, connected with sensuality, enticing.
an6.63:8.9: Yet, *bhikkhus*, in the noble *vinaya* these are not called sensual pleasures — they are called strands of sensuality —
an6.63:9.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the origin of sensual pleasures?
